summary: Policy-backed Windows Update disable control. The app writes the documented DisableWindowsUpdateAccess and NoAutoUpdate policy values; the older pause-timestamp behavior remains in the deprecated parent audit trail.

setting.name: Windows Update Policy Control
setting.casual_explanation: These policies control whether Windows Update access is removed and whether automatic updates are prohibited.
setting.why_people_change_it: People use these policies when they want to block normal Windows Update access or keep automatic updates turned off on managed devices.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Blocking Windows Update can prevent unwanted updates, but it also reduces the device's access to security and feature updates.
setting.professional_notes: ICM.admx documents DisableWindowsUpdateAccess under HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\WindowsUpdate with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0. WindowsUpdate.admx documents NoAutoUpdate under HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\WindowsUpdate\AU with the expected 1/0 mapping.

allowed_values.label: Not configured
allowed_values.meaning: Windows Update access is not blocked by this specific policy.

allowed_values.label: Remove access to Windows Update
allowed_values.meaning: Windows Update features and access points are removed by policy.

allowed_values.label: Automatic updating policy enabled
allowed_values.meaning: The Configure Automatic Updates policy is enabled and AUOptions determines behavior.

allowed_values.label: Automatic updates turned off
allowed_values.meaning: Automatic updates are prohibited by this policy.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes only the documented official policy values in this child record.

windows_defaults.label: Windows policy default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows systems supporting these Windows Update policies

states.rationale: Leave the Windows Update access-removal policy unset.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows managed default
intended_for: Systems that should keep receiving updates normally
avoid_for: Managed devices intentionally blocking Windows Update

states.rationale: Do not apply the access-removal policy.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want to keep Windows Update available.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want to force updates off.
tradeoffs: The app's pause-timestamp behavior is not included in this child record.

recommended_profiles.label: Disable Windows Update Policy
intended_for: Managed devices that should not expose Windows Update access
avoid_for: Systems that must keep receiving updates normally

states.rationale: Remove access to Windows Update by policy.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You need both official policy controls in a managed environment.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You are only documenting the old composite bundle.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft ICM.admx mapping for DisableWindowsUpdateAccess
evidence.summary: The local ADMX file maps DisableWindowsUpdateAccess with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft WindowsUpdate.admx mapping
evidence.summary: The local ADMX file documents NoAutoUpdate under the AU policy key and shows the official Enable/Disable mapping for Configure Automatic Updates.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft WindowsUpdate.adml help text
evidence.summary: The local ADML help text explains DisableWindowsUpdateAccess, NoAutoUpdate, and the driver-exclusion policy, and clarifies that NoAutoUpdate = 1 prohibits Windows from searching for updates.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes DisableWindowsUpdateAccess = 1 and NoAutoUpdate = 1 for this child record.

decision.why: The official policy controls are documented and the app now writes only the policy values in this child record.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: NoAutoUpdate under the AU policy key with the expected 1/0 mapping for Configure Automatic Updates.
validation_proof.notes: The deprecated parent retains the pause-timestamp behavior; this child records the official policy half only.
